name: Apple and Almond Macaroon Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons Vanilla extract or, cognac vanilla
4 large Tart apples, peeled, cored and thinly sliced
1 teaspoon Vanilla extract or, cognac vanilla
recipeInstructions:
To make cognac vanilla, obtain a fresh vanilla bean and cut it lengthwise and crosswise to expose the seeds. Place in an airtight container with 2.5 oz. cognac or brandy per bean. Shake every 2-3 days until desired flavor is achieved.
Preheat oven to 375°F
Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a heavy saucepan. Add flour and blend well until smooth. Slowly add milk, stirring constantly over medium heat; cook until thickened (about 1 minute). Add 3/4 cup sugar and beat hard. Add egg yolks, blending thoroughly. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla, Calvados or brandy.
Melt remaining butter in a skillet. Sauté apples with remaining sugar for 7-8 minutes until tender.
Beat egg whites with salt until stiff peaks form. Fold a small amount into the egg yolk mixture to lighten slightly, then fold in the rest of the whites.
Place half the apples in a buttered shallow baking dish. Cover with half the egg mixture. Add remaining apples and sprinkle with macaroon crumbs. Cover with remaining egg mixture, filling almost to rim.
Bake until top is golden brown and pudding firm (25-30 minutes). Serve immediately with Vanilla Sauce.
Vanilla Sauce:
Combine sugar and cornstarch in a saucepan and mix well. Add salt, lemon peel, and water. Bring to boil and cook until clear (5-7 minutes). Discard lemon peel, add butter, and stir in vanilla. For creamier texture, add 1-2 tablespoons heavy whipping cream.
